referenceFormulas.H2: =INDEX(INDEX(国籍リスト!$B$2:$T$1000, 0, MATCH($B$1, 国籍リスト!$B$1:$T$1, 0)), RANDBETWEEN(1, COUNTA(INDEX(国籍リスト!$B$2:$T$1000, 0, MATCH($B$1, 国籍リスト!$B$1:$T$1, 0)))))

マレーシア.surnameNote: Malay and many Indian Malaysian entries are patronymic/name elements rather than hereditary surnames.

香港.surnameNote: Cantonese-style romanization; spelling varies by family and document.

ポーランド.surnameNote: Many adjective-form surnames have masculine and feminine forms.

ハンガリー.surnameNote: Hungarian domestic order usually places the family name first.

エチオピア.surnameNote: No hereditary surname in the usual Western sense; this pool is for the father's personal name/patronymic slot.

ケニア.surnameNote: Multiethnic pool; keep subgroup consistent when combining.

タンザニア.surnameNote: Multiethnic pool; subgroup labels are broad approximations.

パキスタン.surnameNote: Family, tribal, clan, title and patronymic identifiers coexist.

ミャンマー.surnameNote: Bamar names generally do not use inherited surnames; this is a reusable name-element pool, not a true surname list.

南アフリカ.surnameNote: Highly multilingual and multiethnic; keep subgroup consistent.

フィリピン.surnameNote: Spanish-derived, indigenous and Chinese Filipino surnames coexist.

バングラデシュ.surnameNote: Fixed hereditary surnames are not universal; family/title/name elements are included.

コンゴ民主.surnameNote: Congolese names may include surname (nom), post-surname/postnom and given name; this pool combines common surname/postnom elements.

スイス.surnameNote: German-, French-, Italian- and Romansh-speaking pools are separated.

ベルギー.surnameNote: Dutch/Flemish-, French/Walloon- and German-speaking pools are separated.

台湾.surnameNote: Taiwan-style spellings vary; this pool uses common conventional romanizations.
